questions.question: What is the default value of flex-direction?
questions.explanation: The default value of flex-direction is row.

questions.question: Which flex-direction value arranges items from right to left?
questions.explanation: row-reverse arranges items horizontally but in reverse order (right to left).

questions.question: With flex-direction: column, where does justify-content align items?
questions.explanation: With column, the main axis is vertical, so justify-content aligns vertically.

questions.question: Which value of flex-direction is best for a vertical navigation sidebar?
questions.explanation: column stacks items vertically, perfect for a sidebar navigation.

questions.question: What happens to justify-content when you change flex-direction from row to column?
options: It goes from horizontal to vertical
It goes from vertical to horizontal
questions.explanation: justify-content follows the main axis, which changes with flex-direction.

questions.question: Which accessibility concern applies to row-reverse and column-reverse?
options: Visual order differs from DOM order
questions.explanation: Reverse values change visual order but DOM (and screen reader) order stays the same.

questions.question: With flex-direction: column-reverse, where does the first item appear?
questions.explanation: column-reverse reverses the column direction, so the first item appears at the bottom.

questions.question: Which property changes the cross axis when flex-direction changes?
options: align-items now aligns on the new cross axis
questions.explanation: When flex-direction changes, the cross axis changes, so align-items aligns along the new cross axis.

questions.question: What does flex-direction: row do to the main axis?
questions.explanation: row sets the main axis horizontally from left to right.

questions.question: If you want items stacked vertically, what flex-direction should you use?
questions.explanation: column stacks flex items vertically.

questions.question: Which browsers support the flex-direction property?
questions.explanation: All modern browsers fully support flex-direction.

questions.question: What is the cross axis when flex-direction is column?
questions.explanation: With column, the main axis is vertical, so the cross axis is horizontal.

questions.question: Which flex-direction value would you use for a right-to-left language layout?
questions.explanation: row-reverse arranges items right-to-left, suitable for RTL layouts.

questions.question: How does flex-direction: column affect the width of flex items?
options: Items take full width by default
questions.explanation: With column direction, stretch is the default align-items value, so items stretch to full width.

questions.question: What does the main axis represent in Flexbox?
options: The primary direction items flow along
questions.explanation: The main axis is the primary direction along which flex items are laid out.

questions.question: With flex-direction: row-reverse, what is the cross axis direction?
questions.explanation: Regardless of row or row-reverse, the cross axis is top to bottom.

questions.question: Which is the correct syntax for flex-direction?
questions.explanation: The correct values are row, row-reverse, column, and column-reverse.

questions.question: What happens to align-items when using flex-direction: column?
options: It aligns horizontally (along the cross axis)
questions.explanation: With column, the cross axis is horizontal, so align-items controls horizontal alignment.

questions.question: Which flex-direction is equivalent to a traditional column layout?
questions.explanation: column mimics the traditional vertical stacking of block elements.

questions.question: What is a common use case for flex-direction: column-reverse?
questions.explanation: column-reverse is useful for chat UIs where newest messages appear at the bottom.
